dataset_identity.title: Facade Spectrum of Balat
dataset_identity.description: An interactive data dashboard and comprehensive dataset exploring the architectural typology, material distribution, and color spectrum of historical facades in the Balat district of Istanbul. The project seamlessly merges geospatial urban data with computer vision and computational color analysis.

spatial_coverage.coordinate_reference_system: EPSG:4326 (WGS84) mapped to EPSG:32635 for distance buffers

methodology_pipeline.name: Data Collection
methodology_pipeline.description: Extraction of raw facade photographs from Google Street View imagery, capturing both the standard urban fabric and symbolic historical landmarks.

methodology_pipeline.name: Perspective Correction (Orthorectification)
methodology_pipeline.description: Raw Street View photographs were orthorectified to eliminate keystone distortion using OpenCV perspective transformation matrices and PyTorch-assisted ControlNet-aux models.

methodology_pipeline.name: Architectural Feature Detection
methodology_pipeline.description: Automated object detection using Roboflow was implemented to identify architectural features (windows, bay windows/cumba), informing the structural rhythm of the facades.

methodology_pipeline.name: Computational Color Extraction
methodology_pipeline.description: Orthorectified images were computationally analyzed using a vibrancy-weighted K-Means clustering algorithm (k=24, 10, 5, 3, 1) via OpenCV to extract the true dominant facade colors, ignoring deep shadows and overexposed skies.

methodology_pipeline.name: Geospatial Alignment
methodology_pipeline.description: Extracted GPS coordinates were buffered and matched to OpenStreetMap building footprints using K-Nearest Neighbors (KNN) spatial joins in GeoPandas.

data_sources.content: Building polygons and street network vectors extracted via OSMnx
Manually curated geographic pins for iconic historical landmarks
2D architectural diagrams synthesized entirely by OpenCV and YOLO object detection models.

license.name: MIT License
license.description: Open access for academic and non-commercial research use.
